checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: Income statement / Cash flow statement FY2009
Depreciation FY2009, explains large NI-CFO gap in a lease/depreciation-heavy business
Income statement / Cash flow statement FY2013 - ratio narrowing over time (structural, depreciation-driven)

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=50,146,000 (2009-12-31) growing to InventoryNet=64,298,000 (2013-12-31), broadly in line with revenue growth

evidence.location: CostOfServices and Revenues FY2013 vs FY2010 - modest, gradual margin drift, not erratic

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.location: Quarterly NetIncomeLoss FY2012 - meaningful seasonal swings consistent with business seasonality, not artificially smoothed

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.location: Allowance for doubtful accounts 2013-12-31 (2009-12-31 value from 0000950123-10-094963)

evidence.quote: ReceivablesNetCurrent=598,661,000 (2009-12-31) growing to ReceivablesNetCurrent=777,370,000 (2013-12-31), +29.9%; implied allowance/receivables ratio fell from ~2.31% to ~2.18%

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filing_date=2012-03-19 (accession 0001193125-12-121535), filed roughly one month after the original 10-K (filing_date=2012-02-16), and used as the source accession for numerous FY2008-FY2011 line items (Assets, Liabilities, Goodwill, IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill, ReceivablesNetCurrent, NetIncomeLoss quarterly 2010, CostOfServices FY2009, Depreciation FY2009, StockholdersEquity 2008-12-31, PropertyPlantAndEquipment, InventoryNet, DebtAndCapitalLeaseObligations, OtherAssetsNoncurrent)
evidence.location: filing_chronology entry '10-K/A' 2012-03-19; cross-referenced against financial_series_point_in_time source accessions
filing_chronology entries 2011-2013 (SEC comment letter correspondence pattern)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data available spanning Revenues FY2008 (5,999,041,000) through Q1 2014 (1,610,737,000), with corresponding balance sheet and cash flow items across the same span
evidence.location: Revenues series 2008-2014Q1; Assets, Liabilities, NetIncomeLoss, CFO series similarly span 2007/2009-2014Q1

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: A 10-K/A (accession 0001193125-12-121535) filed 2012-03-19, only ~1 month after the original FY2011 10-K, was used as the reporting source for a broad swath of FY2008-FY2011 balance sheet and income statement figures (Assets=6,259,830,000 (2009-12-31), Goodwill=216,444,000 (2009-12-31), IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=72,269,000 (2010-12-31), NetIncomeLoss quarterly figures for 2010). This pattern is consistent with a restatement/correction of previously issued financial statements (ASC 250) or a measurement-period adjustment to a business combination (ASC 805) that materially changed goodwill/intangible asset allocation and related balances versus what was originally reported.

rationale_evidence.quote: Goodwill=355,842,000 (2010-12-31, per 0000085961-13-000007) vs Goodwill=216,286,000 (2010-06-30, per 0000950123-10-067673) - implying a large goodwill step-up recognized only in the year-end/restated figures
10-K/A filing_date=2012-03-19 used as accession source for Assets 2009-12-31=6,259,830,000, Liabilities 2010-12-31=5,248,061,000, StockholdersEquity 2008-12-31=1,345,161,000

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Broad restatement of prior period financial statement line items across FY2008-FY2011 (evidenced by extensive re-sourcing of historical data to the 2012-03-19 10-K/A rather than original filings), suggesting a correction of accounting errors that shifted recognition of income, equity, or liabilities between periods.

rationale_evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss quarterly data for 2010 (Q1=12,373,000; Q2=29,841,000; Q3=38,835,000; Q4=37,121,000) all sourced from the 10-K/A filed 2012-03-19 rather than contemporaneous 10-Qs

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Allowance for doubtful accounts as a percentage of gross receivables declined modestly over 2009-2013 despite receivables growth, potentially reflecting an under-provisioned bad debt reserve relative to a growing receivables base (understated allowance/bad debt expense).

rationale_evidence.location: Allowance and receivables comparison FY2009 vs FY2013

top_signals: 10-K/A filed 2012-03-19 (accession 0001193125-12-121535), filed roughly a month after the original 10-K, serves as the source for numerous restated FY2008-FY2011 balance sheet and income statement figures - strong indicator of a financial restatement
Sustained SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD comment-letter correspondence clustered in 2011-2013, coincident with the restatement period
Goodwill (+64.4%) and intangible assets (+84.7%) jumped materially faster than revenue (+5.1%) in FY2010, concurrent with the items later restated
Allowance for doubtful accounts coverage ratio to receivables declined slightly (2.31%→2.18%) from 2009 to 2013 despite receivable growth
Persistent large NI-to-CFO gap is structurally explained by heavy depreciation typical of a vehicle leasing business, and its narrowing trend over time is not itself concerning
